title: Non-null assertion on currentNote in classifyCurrentSession for editing/save-failed states
description: In classify-current-session.ts lines 40 and 49, `currentNote!` is used with a non-null assertion. If a caller violates the precondition by passing `currentNote: null` when state.status is 'editing' or 'save-failed', this will propagate null into the CurrentSessionDecision.note field, causing a deferred runtime error rather than a fail-fast. While the spec says 'SavingState and SwitchingState are NOT valid inputs; the caller must guard', there is no equivalent guard documented for null currentNote on editing/save-failed states. The classification table (REQ-EPNS-007) shows `currentNote: Note (non-null)` for save-failed, but the function signature accepts `Note | null`.
recommendation: Add a runtime guard: if `currentNote === null` when `state.status` is 'editing' or 'save-failed', throw an explicit invariant error rather than silently propagating null. This makes the precondition visible and debuggable.
